title: 30-Day Foundation
subtitle: Build the discipline you lost
description: Thirty days. One challenge each day. No excuses, no shortcuts. This program is the starting line, whether you are rebuilding from zero or just getting back on track. By day 30, discipline won't be something you find. It'll be something you are.

days.challengeTitle: Delete the app you waste the most time on
days.challengeDescription: You know which one it is. You don't even have to think about it. Delete it for the next 30 days. You can reinstall it on Day 31. If you hesitate, that's your answer.

days.challengeTitle: Have one conversation you have been avoiding
days.challengeDescription: Not a text. A real conversation, phone call or in person. The one you keep postponing. The longer you wait, the heavier it gets. Do it today.

days.challengeTitle: Spend 20 minutes outside with no phone
days.challengeDescription: Walk, sit, stand. Doesn't matter. No podcast, no music, no scrolling. Just you and your thoughts for 20 minutes. Most men haven't done this in years.

days.challengeTitle: Write down every recurring expense you pay for
days.challengeDescription: Subscriptions, memberships, everything. Total it up. Most men are paying for 4-6 things they don't use. Awareness first, action later.

days.challengeTitle: Do 100 pushups before the day ends
days.challengeDescription: Not all at once. Break it up however you want. 10 sets of 10, 20 sets of 5, whatever it takes. Just hit 100. Don't negotiate with yourself.

days.challengeTitle: Cook a real meal from scratch
days.challengeDescription: No delivery, no microwave, no ready meals. Buy the ingredients, cook the food, eat it. Taking care of what you put in your body is a basic act of self-respect.

days.challengeTitle: Write one page about where you want to be in 12 months
days.challengeDescription: Not goals. Not bullet points. A page of actual writing, like you are describing your life to a friend. Where do you live, what are you doing, how do you feel. Get specific.

days.challengeTitle: Take a full cold shower, minimum 90 seconds
days.challengeDescription: Turn it all the way cold. Stay in for 90 seconds minimum. Your mind will tell you to get out at 20 seconds. Stay anyway. This is practice for every other hard thing.

days.challengeTitle: No alcohol today
days.challengeDescription: One day. If one day feels hard, that's information worth having.

days.challengeTitle: Learn one thing you have been meaning to figure out for months
days.challengeDescription: That skill, concept, tool or topic you keep saying you'll look into. Spend 45 minutes actually learning it today. Not watching videos about it. Learning it.

days.challengeTitle: Clean out your phone contacts
days.challengeDescription: Go through every name. For each one ask: would I call this person if I needed help? If no, delete. Your network should be real, not just a list of people you once met.

days.challengeTitle: Run or walk 5km (3 miles) without stopping
days.challengeDescription: Pace doesn't matter. Stopping does. Pick a route, go, finish it. Your body will find reasons to quit. Give it none.

days.challengeTitle: Write down the three biggest mistakes of the last two years
days.challengeDescription: Not to beat yourself up. To look at them clearly. Most men avoid doing this, which is exactly why they keep repeating them. Write them down, read them back, then close the notebook.

days.challengeTitle: Do something generous for someone with no reason and no announcement
days.challengeDescription: No posting about it. No expecting anything back. Pay for someone's coffee, help a neighbor, handle a task for someone without being asked. Generosity you keep quiet is the only kind that matters.

days.challengeTitle: Spend 45 minutes on a creative project you abandoned
days.challengeDescription: The music, the writing, the business idea, the thing you stopped. 45 minutes. Not to finish it. Just to show up for it again.

days.challengeTitle: No complaints for the entire day
days.challengeDescription: Every time you are about to complain, stop. Reframe or say nothing. Count how many times you catch yourself. This is one of the hardest days in this program and it should be.

days.challengeTitle: Wake up one hour earlier than usual and protect that hour
days.challengeDescription: Not for work. For you. Read, move, think, plan. No phone for that first hour. The person who owns their morning owns their day. Try it once and see.

days.challengeTitle: Do a workout that actually challenges you
days.challengeDescription: Not a walk. Not a light session. Something that makes you question whether you can finish it. 45 minutes minimum. You should feel it tomorrow.

days.challengeTitle: Cut out sugar completely for 24 hours
days.challengeDescription: No added sugar, no sweetened drinks, no dessert, no hidden sugar in sauces. One day of total control. Proves you have it.

days.challengeTitle: Apologize to someone you owe one to
days.challengeDescription: You know who. Not a text message. A real apology with no excuses attached. You don't get to know how they'll respond. That's not the point.

days.challengeTitle: Spend one full hour with your phone in another room
days.challengeDescription: No exceptions, no quick checks. One hour of being unreachable. Notice what you feel. Boredom, anxiety, relief. All of it is useful information.

days.challengeTitle: Read 50 pages of a non-fiction book in one sitting
days.challengeDescription: Not an article. Not a summary. A real book, 50 pages, no interruptions. If you don't own one, that's today's first task.

days.challengeTitle: Write your personal rules
days.challengeDescription: Not goals. Rules. Things you will and won't do, regardless of how you feel. Minimum five, maximum ten. These become your operating system. Write them like you mean them.

days.challengeTitle: Do 200 bodyweight reps today
days.challengeDescription: Pushups, squats, lunges, dips. Mix it however you want. Hit 200 total. Track every single rep. Your body is capable of more than your mind will tell you.

days.challengeTitle: Tell someone in your life something true that you have been holding back
days.challengeDescription: Not to hurt anyone. Something honest that you have been swallowing. A boundary, a feeling, an appreciation you never expressed. One true thing said out loud.

days.challengeTitle: Eliminate your single biggest time-wasting habit for 24 hours
days.challengeDescription: You already know what it is. The thing you do when you are avoiding something real. One day without it. See what fills the space. That thing is probably more important.

days.challengeTitle: Cook every single meal you eat today from scratch
days.challengeDescription: Breakfast, lunch, dinner. No exceptions. Treating your body like it deserves real food is not optional, it is the baseline.

days.challengeTitle: Write a brutally honest self-assessment
days.challengeDescription: Two columns. What you are doing well. What you are still avoiding. No softening, no excuses. This is the gap between who you are and who you said you wanted to be on Day 7. Close it.

days.challengeTitle: Do one thing today that the version of you from Day 1 couldn't have done
days.challengeDescription: You decide what that is. Physical, social, mental. You know yourself better than this app does. Pick something that proves something to yourself.

days.challengeTitle: Write a letter to the person who starts this program next
days.challengeDescription: What would you tell them? What was harder than expected? What surprised you? What actually changed? You don't have to send it to anyone. Write it like you mean it. This is the last day. You showed up.
